summary: WebSocket keyword detection NOT WORKING on copilot.microsoft.com. Both 'password' (EN) and '비밀번호' (KR) keywords passed through WebSocket without blocking. Copilot responded normally to both messages via WSS.

test_results.detail: Copilot responded normally with password security advice. WebSocket connection (wss://copilot.microsoft.com/c/api/chat?api-version=2) remained active. Status 101 Switching Protocols. No disconnection or blocking observed. on_upgraded_data() did NOT trigger block_session().
Copilot responded normally in Korean with password management advice. WebSocket connection still active. No blocking, no error, no disconnection.

analysis.ws_upgrade_intercepted_by_etap: UNKNOWN - upgrade succeeded (101), suggesting etap either passed it through or on_upgraded_data() callback is not triggering keyword check on this service
possible_causes: on_upgraded_data() may not be matching copilot.microsoft.com domain in APF service list
WS frame payload may be in a format that keyword scanner does not parse (e.g., binary frames, compressed, or JSON-wrapped)
The keyword check may only scan HTTP POST body, not WS frame data
copilot.microsoft.com may not be registered for WS keyword inspection in the APF database
